topic_title: The 70/30 rule — AI implements, you specify and verify
verdict: PASS — all six checks pass. One marginal length warning noted (corpus estimated ~2100 words, at the 30%-below-standard-floor boundary of 2100; not a hard fail). No hard failures. Corpus is well-structured, tightly scoped, fully cited, and beginner-accessible.

citation_density.detail: All 3 resources (1 primary, 2 supporting) cited at least once. Citation markers appear on virtually every substantive factual claim in Core Concepts, Implementation, and Real-World Patterns. Density well above 60% threshold.

sequence_drift.detail: All technical terms used are either introduced by this topic or covered in prior topics 1.1–2.4. References to 'probabilistic systems' (1.3), 'testable/bounded/observable/actionable' (2.1), 'six-component checklist / failure conditions' (2.3) are correct back-references. 'AI diagnostic assistant', 'data journalism', and 'software testing' in section 7 are illustrative real-world examples, not new technical concepts being defined or taught. No forward-referenced terms detected.

length_sanity.detail: Estimated word count of ~2107 words is at the exact 30%-below-standard boundary (hard floor = 2100). The corpus is below the standard profile target range of 3000–4400 words. This is a borderline marginal warning, not a hard fail, because the estimate is within the 30% buffer margin. The builder should consider expanding Core Concepts (section 5) and/or adding worked examples to bring the corpus closer to the 3000-word standard-profile target before artifact generation.

topic_scope.detail: The corpus stays tightly within the topic headline. All five subsections of Core Concepts (5.1–5.5), Implementation (section 6), Real-World Patterns (section 7), and Best Practices (section 8) directly address the 70/30 rule and its two human roles (specify, verify). Section 9 references Assessment A1 as context only — no new content taught. No adjacent or more-advanced concepts (e.g., prompt engineering, AI governance, hallucination mechanics) are defined or taught at depth.

beginner_readability.detail: All non-everyday terms ('70/30 rule', 'implementation', 'verification', 'specification') are defined on first use with plain-English meanings. The corpus introduces one concept per section. 'Probabilistic' is re-used from topic 1.3 and correctly flagged as prior learning ('you learned this in Topic 1.3') rather than re-defined — acceptable under curriculum rules. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously. No hedging phrases detected. Sentences are short, active-voice, and address the learner as 'you'. Fewer than 3 distinct readability findings — no escalation to hard fail.

summary_findings.detail: Estimated ~2107 words is at the lower boundary of the 30%-buffer hard floor for the standard profile (target 3000–4400 words). Recommend expanding before artifact generation to ensure the reading artifact can meet its own standard-profile word count.
